title: Cam and Follower Mechanism — Displacement Profiles
content: A cam converts rotary motion to controlled linear or oscillating motion via a follower. Follower displacement is governed by the cam profile. Common profiles: uniform velocity (triangular), simple harmonic, cycloidal, modified sine.
extended_content: Cycloidal profile minimises acceleration jerk (infinite jerk → fatigue). Max follower acceleration a_max = 2πh/β² × ω² (cycloidal) where h = lift, β = cam angle, ω = angular velocity. Pressure angle φ: tan(φ) = dy/dθ / (R_base + y), must stay < 30° to avoid excessive side thrust on follower. For ventilator bellows cam: eccentric disc (simple harmonic) gives smooth piston displacement, lift = stroke = tidal volume/piston_area ≈ 400–600 mL / 50 cm² = 8–12 mm. Cam RPM = breaths/min = 12–20. Return spring force > inertia + load. Cam material: hardened steel 50–60 HRC follower pad required.
units: a: m/s², h: m, ω: rad/s, β: rad, φ: deg
source: Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design, 10th ed., Ch. 16; Norton, Design of Machinery, 5th ed.; Rothbart, Cam Design Handbook
